pre_execution_gate.instruction: Before writing ANY code, read all attached files, then produce a summary of your understanding in the chat. The summary MUST include: 1. Task scope (what you will benchmark and why) 2. Test matrix dimensions (NAT values × bands × runs) 3. Evidence artifact path 4. Quality gate criteria 5. Governance updates required 6. Pre-condition verification checklist Wait for Lead Architect approval before proceeding.

pre_conditions.check: Branch: `feature/p5-task4-3-nat-sweep` created from main HEAD (after sequential merges of task4-2-combined + eagle3-gap-fill). Verify HEAD contains Task 4.2 evidence and EAGLE-3 gap fill commits.

ADR-012 §2.2 "Draft model" row shows **LOCKED** (not EVALUATING). If still EVALUATING after merge, update to: `| **Draft model** | Qwen3-0.6B INT4 (28 layers, 1024 hidden) | **LOCKED** | Task 4.2: T-09 = 13.77 tps / 3.18x speedup. Draft-B eliminated. |`
Python .venv active with openvino_genai, psutil, transformers.
Test suite baseline: run `python -m pytest --tb=short -q --no-header` and record pass count. No test failures expected. Task 4.3 adds NO unit tests (benchmark harness only).

scope.summary: Run a comprehensive NAT sweep across the full production context range using the LOCKED draft model (Qwen3-0.6B 28L INT4 GPU) with the LOCKED target model (Qwen3-14B INT4 GPU). Determine the optimal `num_assistant_tokens` value globally and per context band. Capture all 7 mandatory per-run measurements. Lock NAT in ADR-012 §2.2 if evidence is conclusive; emit INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE if not.
scope.rationale: P5-005b found NAT=3 optimal at 4K with Draft-A, but that sweep was limited (NAT=[3,5,7,10] at 4K only). The original §4.3 spec required NAT sweep × 2 draft models × 4K. With Draft-B now eliminated (Task 4.2: DRAFT_A_WINS) and Draft-A LOCKED, the NAT sweep focuses on a single draft model across the full production context range. At long context (12K-16K), target verification passes are slower, making each accepted draft token worth more wall-clock time — the optimal NAT may be HIGHER than at 4K. Conversely, draft acceptance rate may degrade at long context. These effects interact non-linearly. Empirical evidence across the full range is required before locking NAT.
scope.harness_declaration: NO_FULL_HARNESS — new script, no prior harness at this sweep scope. Pattern derived from run_p5_task4_2_combined.py (corrected list-input generate, extended_perf_metrics extraction).
scope.no_production_code_changes: Task 4.3 touches phase2_gates/ and docs/ ONLY. No modifications to services/, shared/, launcher/, or tests/.

dimension.name: NAT
dimension.note: NAT=1,2: lower bound validation (is NAT=1 faster than baseline? where does draft overhead become worthwhile?) NAT=3: P5-005b provisional best at 4K / confirms or challenges NAT=5: original spec re-test value NAT=7: performance ceiling probe NAT=10: diminishing returns boundary (P5-005b: 8.22 tps at 4K)

dimension.name: prompt_band
dimension.note: 512/2048: PA-relevant short context bands 4096: baseline from P5-005b, primary comparison point 8192-16384: AO + Code Agent production range 20480: ceiling validation (OPTIONAL — graceful OOM)

test_matrix.execution_order:
Band-outer, NAT-inner. For each band, construct the prompt ONCE, then sweep all 6 NAT values with that prompt. This ensures: - Prompt construction cost is amortized (1 tokenization per band) - Thermal state is comparable across NAT values within a band - If OOM occurs at 20K, it fails per-NAT-value (not entire band)
Order: [512→2048→4096→8192→12288→16384→20480] × [NAT=1→2→3→5→7→10]

pipeline_config.construction: scheduler = SchedulerConfig() scheduler.cache_size = 3 # GB (SCHEDULER_CACHE_GB) pipeline = LLMPipeline( str(target_path), "GPU", scheduler_config=scheduler, draft_model=ov_genai.draft_model(str(draft_path), "GPU"), ) # Do NOT set INFERENCE_PRECISION — invalid property name on this OV build. # Do NOT set GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION — XAttention OFF is default. # FP16 is the Xe2/Arc 140V default.
pipeline_config.note: Pipeline is compiled ONCE. NAT is a GenerationConfig parameter — vary it per-run without recompiling the pipeline.

test_matrix.generation_config: # Per-run, parameterized by NAT value: cfg = ov_genai.GenerationConfig() cfg.max_new_tokens = 128 cfg.do_sample = False cfg.temperature = 0.0 cfg.top_k = 1 cfg.top_p = 1.0 cfg.num_assistant_tokens = NAT_VALUE # varies: 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10 cfg.assistant_confidence_threshold = 0.0
test_matrix.prompt_construction: Use transformers AutoTokenizer for Qwen3-14B. System prompt: "You are a helpful assistant." User content: padded to target token count per band. Apply chat template with add_generation_prompt=True. Build prompt ONCE per band, reuse across NAT values.
test_matrix.band_20k_oom_handling: The 20,480 band is OPTIONAL. Harness MUST: 1. Save intermediate results to disk after EACH band completes (bands 512-16384 are preserved regardless of what happens at 20K). 2. Wrap each NAT-at-20K in try/except catching RuntimeError, MemoryError, and any Exception subclass. 3. If OOM detected for a NAT value at 20K: - Mark that config as status="OOM_SKIPPED" - Record the exception fingerprint - Continue to next NAT value at 20K (do NOT abort entire band) - Call gc.collect() between attempts 4. If ALL NAT values OOM at 20K, mark entire band as "OOM_ALL_SKIPPED". 5. Session is NOT a failure if any or all 20K configs OOM. 6. If process is at risk of hard crash (e.g., RSS exceeds 26,000 MB warning), abort 20K band immediately.

per_run.field: Wall-clock tokens/second for full target+draft pipeline. Source: wall-clock timing + token count, OR extended_perf_metrics.get_throughput().mean
Mean draft model forward pass time within speculative pipeline. Source: extended_perf_metrics.draft_model_metrics.get_inference_duration().mean
NAT × total_speculative_episodes. Source: raw_metrics.m_batch_sizes count × NAT
sum(b - 1 for b in m_batch_sizes). Source: raw_metrics.m_batch_sizes
Per-step array [step_1_rate, ..., step_NAT_rate]. Source: m_batch_sizes analysis (see extract_acceptance_metrics pattern in Task 4.2 harness)
Process RSS after warmup. Source: RssSampler thread.
First-token wall-clock time. Source: stream_cb timestamp or extended_perf_metrics.get_ttft().mean

supplementary.field: Draft model generating tokens alone (no target). Run 3 times at 4K context, report mean. One-time measurement, not per-config.

mandatory_measurements.validation_rule: A run missing ANY of the 7 fields is INVALID and must not be counted. If valid_count drops below 3 for any config, mark that config as INSUFFICIENT_DATA.

api_critical_patterns.pattern: pipeline.generate([prompt], gen_config, streamer_callback) NEVER: pipeline.generate(prompt, gen_config, streamer_callback) Bare str input returns bare str with NO perf_metrics, NO extended_perf_metrics. List input returns DecodedResults with .texts[0], .perf_metrics, .extended_perf_metrics.

output = pipeline.generate([prompt], gc, cb) epm = output.extended_perf_metrics # SDPerModelsPerfMetrics epm.get_throughput().mean # native combined TPS epm.get_ttft().mean # native TTFT (ms) epm.draft_model_metrics.get_throughput().mean # draft TPS epm.draft_model_metrics.get_inference_duration().mean # draft forward ms epm.main_model_metrics.get_throughput().mean # target TPS epm.get_num_accepted_tokens() # integer accepted count

raw = output.perf_metrics.raw_metrics batch_sizes = list(raw.m_batch_sizes) # batch_sizes[i] = number of tokens accepted+1 for episode i # Per-step acceptance: for step k (1-indexed), count episodes where b >= k+1 # Aggregate: sum(b-1 for b in batch_sizes) / (NAT × len(batch_sizes))

quality_gate.gate: All 7 mandatory fields present for all measured runs at bands [512..16384]. 20K band exempt (OOM acceptable). Threshold: zero invalid runs at bands 512-16384.
valid_count >= 5 for all non-OOM configurations. If any config at bands 512-16384 has valid_count < 5, disposition = INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE.
For each band, identify the NAT value with highest mean combined_tps. Record: best_nat, best_tps, second_best_nat, delta_tps, delta_percent.

Compute the NAT value that wins (highest TPS) at the most bands. If one NAT wins at ALL bands: recommend LOCKED at that value. If NAT varies by band: document per-band winners and compute a weighted recommendation (weight by use-case relevance: PA bands 512-4K weight=0.3, AO bands 4K-16K weight=0.5, Code Agent 8K-20K weight=0.2).

IF optimal NAT differs by context band: - Document the data (per-band best NAT table) - Compute the TPS cost of using a single global NAT at each band (e.g., if global=3 but 16K-optimal=5, what is the TPS delta at 16K?) - If TPS cost of global NAT exceeds 10% at ANY band, flag for SDO review - The Execution Agent does NOT lock NAT in this case — SDO decides: Option A: single global NAT (simpler, one GenConfig for all profiles) Option B: per-range adaptive NAT (e.g., NAT=3 for bands ≤4K, NAT=5 for ≥8K) - Record the recommendation but mark disposition as SDO_DECISION_REQUIRED.

For the winning NAT value, plot acceptance_rate_aggregate across bands. If acceptance rate drops below 0.25 at any band ≤16K, flag as ACCEPTANCE_DEGRADATION_WARNING (draft model may be mismatched at long context).
peak_rss_mb at the highest successful band must be < 15,507 MB. If exceeded, flag MEMORY_BUDGET_EXCEEDED.
quality_gate.disposition_rules: ALL gates G-01 through G-07 PASS + single NAT wins all bands: → disposition = NAT_LOCKED, update ADR-012 §2.2 ALL gates PASS + NAT varies by band: → disposition = SDO_DECISION_REQUIRED, do NOT update ADR ANY gate G-01/G-02 FAIL: → disposition = INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE G-07 FAIL: → disposition = MEMORY_BUDGET_EXCEEDED (session still valid, flag for SDO)

evidence_artifact.schema: { "milestone": "P5-TASK-4.3", "timestamp_utc": "ISO-8601", "metadata": { "commit_hash": "git HEAD", "python_version": "...", "platform": "...", "openvino_version": "...", "openvino_genai_version": "...", "power_envelope": { ... }, "memory_limits": { "warning_rss_mb": 26000.0, "budget_rss_mb": 15507.0 } }, "benchmark_policy": { "nat_values": [1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10], "prompt_bands": [512, 2048, 4096, 8192, 12288, 16384, 20480], "warmup_runs": 2, "measured_runs": 5, "max_new_tokens": 128, "scheduler_cache_gb": 3, "xattention": "OFF (default)", "pipeline_construction": "SchedulerConfig" }, "pipeline_compile_ms": 12345.6, "standalone_draft_tps": { "mean": ..., "runs": [...] }, "results": [ { "band": 512, "nat": 1, "status": "completed | OOM_SKIPPED | INSUFFICIENT_DATA", "warmup_runs": 2, "measured_runs": [ ... per-run 7-field records ... ], "summary": { "combined_tps": { "mean": ..., "stddev": ..., "p50": ..., "p95": ... }, "ttft_ms": { "mean": ..., "stddev": ... }, "draft_forward_ms_per_step": { "mean": ... }, "tokens_drafted_total": ..., "tokens_accepted_total": ..., "acceptance_rate_aggregate": ..., "acceptance_rate_by_step": [step_1, step_2, ...], "peak_rss_mb": ... } } // ... 42 entries total (6 NAT × 7 bands) ], "analysis": { "best_nat_per_band": { "512": { "nat": 3, "tps": ... }, ... }, "global_nat_recommendation": { "nat": 3, "win_count": 7, "method": "..." }, "adaptive_nat_needed": false, "acceptance_rate_trend": { ... per-band AR for winning NAT ... } }, "quality_gate": { "G-01": "PASS|FAIL", "G-02": "PASS|FAIL", "G-03": "PASS|FAIL", "G-04": "PASS|FAIL", "G-05": "PASS|FAIL|N/A", "G-06": "PASS|FAIL", "G-07": "PASS|FAIL", "disposition": "NAT_LOCKED | SDO_DECISION_REQUIRED | INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE", "locked_nat_value": null // or integer if disposition=NAT_LOCKED }, "finished_utc": "ISO-8601" }

governance_updates.update: Update ADR-012 §2.2 `num_assistant_tokens` row: - Status: **PROVISIONAL BEST** → **LOCKED** - Best Known: [winning NAT value] - Notes: "Task 4.3: NAT=[value] wins at all bands (512-20K). TPS=[best] at 4K. Evidence: p5_task4_3_nat_sweep_matrix.json"
Do NOT update ADR-012 §2.2. Leave as PROVISIONAL BEST. Add a note: "Task 4.3: NAT varies by band — SDO decision pending. See evidence artifact for per-band analysis."

ADR-012 §2.2 Pipeline construction row: - If currently EVALUATING: update Notes to "Task 4.3 used SchedulerConfig (consistent with Task 4.2). Dict-config comparison deferred — SchedulerConfig is the de facto standard across all Task 4 harnesses." (Do NOT change status to LOCKED — that requires explicit SDO decision.)

Append new LEDGER entry to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. Use the NEXT available entry number (count existing ### Entry N headers + 1). Format:
### Entry [N] — Task 4.3: NAT Sweep × Context Bands
**Date:** 2026-03-0X (actual execution date) **Branch:** `feature/p5-task4-3-nat-sweep` **Commit:** `[HEAD after commit]` **Type:** Empirical Evidence Collection (Configuration Optimization) **Scope:** NAT sweep [1,2,3,5,7,10] × 7 prompt bands [512..20480] using LOCKED draft model (Qwen3-0.6B 28L INT4 GPU) + Qwen3-14B target.
Key findings: - [Best global NAT and TPS] - [Per-band analysis summary] - [Acceptance rate trend] - [Memory budget status] - [20K band status]
Quality gate: [G-01..G-07 results] Disposition: [NAT_LOCKED / SDO_DECISION_REQUIRED / INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE]
Evidence: `phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_3_nat_sweep_matrix.json`

execution_prompt.intermediate_save_strategy: The harness MUST save partial results after each band completes. Use an intermediate file: `p5_task4_3_nat_sweep_matrix.json.partial` After all bands complete (or 20K OOM), rename to final artifact path. This ensures bands 512-16384 data survives a 20K hard crash.
execution_prompt.commit_template:
feat(benchmark): Task 4.3 — NAT sweep × context bands (production config)
Sweep num_assistant_tokens [1,2,3,5,7,10] across prompt bands [512,2048,4096,8192,12288,16384,20480] with LOCKED draft model (Qwen3-0.6B 28L INT4 GPU) + Qwen3-14B target.
- 42 configurations, 5 measured runs each, 2 warmup discarded - All 7 mandatory per-run measurements captured - Disposition: [NAT_LOCKED at NAT=X | SDO_DECISION_REQUIRED] - ADR-012 §2.2 updated: num_assistant_tokens [LOCKED|PROVISIONAL] - LEDGER Entry [N] - 20K band: [completed | OOM_SKIPPED]
Evidence: phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_3_nat_sweep_matrix.json

baseline.name: P5-005b NAT sweep at 4K (Draft-A, single band)

reference_data.baseline: NAT=3: 10.72 tps | NAT=5: 10.02 tps | NAT=7: 10.65 tps | NAT=10: 8.22 tps
T-09: 13.77 tps, 3.18x speedup, AR=aggregate from m_batch_sizes
reference_data.note: P5-005b and Task 4.2 used different harness versions. TPS values are directionally comparable but may differ by ~10-20% due to harness differences (warmup handling, prompt content, measurement method). Task 4.3 establishes its own internal baseline — cross-session comparison is informational only.

execution_prompt.runtime_estimate: Pipeline compilation: ~20 seconds (once) Prompt construction: ~5 seconds per band × 7 = ~35 seconds Generate calls: ~294 total 512 band: 42 calls × ~6 sec = ~4 min 2048 band: 42 calls × ~8 sec = ~6 min 4096 band: 42 calls × ~12 sec = ~8 min 8192 band: 42 calls × ~18 sec = ~13 min 12288 band: 42 calls × ~25 sec = ~18 min 16384 band: 42 calls × ~32 sec = ~22 min 20480 band: 42 calls × ~40 sec = ~28 min (if no OOM) Total: ~100-120 minutes (~1.7-2.0 hours) Well within 3-4 hour single-session ceiling.
execution_prompt.attachment_order: 1. .github/copilot-instructions.md (governance — MUST) 2. docs/Task4.3_v1.xml (this prompt — PRIMARY) 3. docs/P5_TASK4_PRODUCTION_CONFIG_FEASIBILITY.md (spec reference — §6 measurements) 4. docs/adrs/ADR-012-Qwen3-14B-Model-Selection-Speculative-Decoding.md (parameter table)
